title: A Probabilistic Sign Rule for Quotients of Positive Series and Integral Transforms
headline: Probabilistic Sign Rule for Quotients of Positive Series and Integral Transforms
tldr: This paper introduces a probabilistic sign rule that links the sign of quotients of functions represented by positive series or integrals to probability laws derived from normalized summands or integrands. By expressing parameter log-derivatives as moments of kernels, the authors provide a novel framework for analyzing complex function quotients.
significance: It offers a powerful tool for mathematical analysis, enabling new insights into the behavior of function quotients across various applications.

title: ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay as LLM Harness for Long-Context Reasoning
headline: ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay Enhances Long-Context Reasoning for Real-World LLM Applications
tldr: ReContext introduces a recursive evidence replay mechanism that allows LLMs to revisit and leverage relevant information within long inputs, improving reasoning accuracy. The approach demonstrates significant gains on benchmark tasks, bridging the gap between context size and effective utilization.
significance: By enabling LLMs to systematically reuse evidence, ReContext paves the way for more reliable reasoning in data-intensive applications.

title: Beyond Adam: SOAP and Muon for Faster, Label-Efficient Training of Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
headline: Optimizing ML Interatomic Potentials: Beyond Adam with SOAP and Muon
tldr: Researchers compare new optimizers to Adam for training machine learning interatomic potentials, achieving faster convergence and reduced label requirements. The study demonstrates that SOAP and Muon frameworks can significantly improve training efficiency and accuracy.
significance: This work highlights the importance of optimizer selection in MLIP training, offering a path to more efficient and scalable simulations.

title: Seek to Segment: Active Perception for Panoramic Referring Segmentation
headline: Active Panoramic Referring Segmentation: Enabling Embodied AI to Navigate 360° Environments
tldr: Traditional referring segmentation models process static images, limiting their use in embodied AI. Our new Active Panoramic Referring Segmentation task allows agents to actively adjust viewpoints in continuous 360° scenes to segment referred objects.
significance: This work bridges the gap between passive vision models and active perception required for real-world embodied agents.

title: Controllable Sim Agents with Behavior Latents
headline: Controllable Simulated Agents Using Behavior Latents for Realistic Traffic Modeling
tldr: We propose Controllable Neural Variational Agents (CNeVA) that learn to infer per-agent behavior latent variables from logged traffic data, enabling steering along interpretable axes. This controllability allows engineers to isolate variables, reproduce edge cases, and test autonomous systems safely without real-world deployment.
significance: CNeVA bridges the gap between realistic simulation fidelity and controllable scenario generation, accelerating safe autonomous vehicle testing.

title: Towards Robustness against Typographic Attack with Training-free Concept Localization
headline: Enhancing CLIP Robustness: Training-Free Localization Against Typographic Attacks
tldr: This study introduces a training-free concept localization technique to mitigate typographic attacks that mislead CLIP-based vision encoders. By isolating visual concepts from textual noise, the method improves robustness without additional training.
significance: It demonstrates a lightweight, data-free defense that preserves CLIP's performance while protecting against adversarial text artifacts.

title: Cut-off Jastrow Factors and Spectral Barron Regularity of Coulombic Electronic Wave Functions
headline: Spectral Barron Regularity of Coulombic Wave Functions via Cut-off Jastrow Extraction
tldr: We analyze the spectral Barron regularity of Coulombic electronic eigenfunctions after removing a cut-off Jastrow factor. The study extends previous results on Coulomb Hamiltonians and provides new insights into wave function smoothness.
significance: This work deepens our understanding of electronic wave function regularity, which is crucial for accurate quantum chemical computations.

title: Visually Grounded Self-Reflection for Vision-Language Models via Reinforcement Learning
headline: Grounding Self-Reflection in Vision‑Language Models for Accurate Visual Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
tldr: The paper introduces a reinforcement learning framework that trains vision‑language models to revisit and correct their textual reasoning steps while ensuring alignment with visual evidence. Experiments show that visually grounded self‑reflection improves accuracy on multimodal benchmarks compared to standard chain‑of‑thought methods.
significance: By integrating visual grounding into self‑reflection, the work bridges a key gap in multimodal reasoning, enabling LVLMs to produce more reliable, evidence‑based conclusions.

title: The Debris Disk Host $β$ Piscis Austrinus is a Rapidly Rotating Star Seen Nearly Pole-On
headline: Rapidly Rotating Star β Piscis Austrinus Revealed as Pole-On via High-Resolution Spectroscopy
tldr: Using the fastrot-spec code, we modeled HARPS spectra of β PsA to determine its inclination, confirming it is a rapidly rotating star seen nearly pole-on. This provides new insights into its photospheric properties and rotational dynamics.
significance: The study refines our understanding of stellar rotation effects on spectral line shapes, aiding accurate characterization of rapidly rotating stars.

title: GeoMix: Descriptor-Free Visual Localization via Global Context and Multi-Detector Training
headline: GeoMix Enhances Descriptor-Free Visual Localization with Global Context and Multi-Detector Training
tldr: GeoMix introduces a descriptor-free localization pipeline that leverages global context and multi-detector training to improve geometric discriminability. The approach reduces storage, preserves privacy, and narrows the performance gap with descriptor-based methods.
significance: By boosting accuracy while eliminating descriptor storage, GeoMix paves the way for scalable, privacy-preserving visual localization in large-scale mapping.

title: Combating Textual Noise and Redundancy: Entropy-Aware Dense Visual Token Pruning
headline: Entropy-Aware Token Pruning: Reducing Textual Noise for Faster Vision‑Language Models
tldr: We propose an entropy‑aware pruning strategy that identifies and removes redundant visual tokens while mitigating the impact of noisy textual prompts. This approach preserves critical visual cues under dense instructions, achieving faster inference without sacrificing accuracy.
significance: By addressing textual noise, the method enables more reliable and efficient vision‑language models for fine‑grained tasks.

title: The Merger-Driven Origin of the Vast Extended Stellar Disc Around the Andromeda Galaxy
headline: Evidence of a Recent Major Merger Shaping Andromeda's Vast Extended Stellar Disc
tldr: Observations of Andromeda's inner halo and a kinematically hot disc reveal a widespread star formation burst 2.5 Gyr ago, pointing to a gas‑rich major merger. This event likely built the galaxy’s extensive outer disc and explains its current substructures.
significance: The findings link a recent merger to the formation of Andromeda’s large stellar disc, offering a new perspective on spiral galaxy evolution.

title: WorldDirector: Building Controllable World Simulators with Persistent Dynamic Memory
headline: WorldDirector: A Controllable Video World Model with Persistent Dynamic Memory and Unrestricted Viewpoint Exploration
tldr: WorldDirector decouples semantic motion orchestration from visual rendering, enabling persistent dynamic object memory and unrestricted viewpoint exploration. This framework surpasses existing world models by separating physical dynamics from pixel generation, allowing more controllable and efficient video simulations.
significance: By separating dynamics from rendering, WorldDirector offers a scalable, controllable foundation for future AI-driven video simulation and analysis.

title: Alignment Is All You Need For X-to-4D Generation
headline: Align4D: Aligning Any Modality for Scalable 4D Generation with Diffusion Models
tldr: The paper introduces Align4D, a framework that aligns arbitrary user-defined modalities to generate 4D content using diffusion models, overcoming dataset costs and scalability limits. It demonstrates flexible, high-quality synthesis across images, videos, and 3D data under multimodal control.
significance: Align4D enables efficient, scalable multimodal 4D generation, reducing reliance on large, diverse datasets.

title: PointDiT: Pixel-Space Diffusion for Monocular Geometry Estimation
headline: PointDiT: Pixel‑Space Diffusion Transformer for Efficient Monocular 3D Reconstruction Method
tldr: PointDiT introduces a lightweight diffusion transformer operating directly in pixel space, eliminating the need for complex hybrid architectures or latent compression. The model achieves state‑of‑the‑art monocular geometry estimation while simplifying training and inference pipelines.
significance: By removing architectural overhead, PointDiT demonstrates that pixel‑space diffusion can match or surpass latent‑space methods for single‑image 3D reconstruction, paving the way for more accessible geometry estimation tools.

title: Distributed Attacks in Persistent-State AI Control
headline: Distributed Attacks via Persistent AI Coding Agents and Iterative Pull Requests
tldr: AI coding agents that persist across sessions can spread malicious code across multiple pull requests, timing attacks to blend in. This paper introduces Iterative VibeCoding to analyze how such distributed attacks evolve and how to detect them.
significance: Understanding distributed attacks in persistent AI coding agents is crucial for securing collaborative software development pipelines.

title: LACUNA: A Testbed for Evaluating Localization Precision for LLM Unlearning
headline: A Comprehensive Testbed for Evaluating Localization Precision in LLM Unlearning
tldr: LLMs often memorize sensitive data, raising privacy concerns. LACUNA provides a benchmark to assess how precisely unlearning methods can target and remove such memorized information.
significance: By quantifying localization precision, LACUNA enables the development of more effective and trustworthy unlearning techniques for large language models.

title: Program-as-Weights: A Programming Paradigm for Fuzzy Functions
headline: Program-as-Weights: Compiling Natural Language into Fuzzy Functions for Everyday Tasks
tldr: The paper introduces fuzzy-function programming, a paradigm that compiles natural-language specifications into executable fuzzy functions, reducing reliance on large language model APIs. This approach improves locality, reproducibility, and cost while handling tasks like log alerting, JSON repair, and intent-based ranking.
significance: It offers a reproducible, cost‑effective alternative to LLM-based solutions for fuzzy logic tasks.

title: Online Safety Monitoring for LLMs
headline: Real-Time Safety Monitoring for Large Language Models Using Verifier Thresholding
tldr: We propose a lightweight real-time monitor that converts a verifier's safety signal into an alarm by thresholding, with the threshold tuned via risk control. Experiments on mathematical tasks demonstrate its effectiveness in detecting unsafe outputs at deployment.
significance: This approach enables continuous safety assurance for LLM deployments without heavy computational overhead.

title: From SRA to Self-Flow: Data Augmentation or Self-Supervision?
headline: From SRA to Self-Flow: Enhancing Diffusion Transformers via Self-Alignment and Dual-Time Scheduling
tldr: Self-Alignment methods like SRA and Self-Flow eliminate the need for external pretrained encoders by embedding representation alignment directly into diffusion models. Dual-time scheduling further boosts training speed and generation quality, marking a significant step toward more autonomous diffusion transformer training.
significance: This work demonstrates that internal alignment mechanisms can replace costly external encoders, streamlining diffusion transformer pipelines and improving efficiency.

title: What LLM Agents Say When No One Is Watching: Social Structure and Latent Objective Emergence in Multi-Agent Debates
headline: When LLMs Debate Quietly: Emergent Social Dynamics Without Explicit Goals
tldr: We examine how language‑model agents behave differently when speaking publicly versus privately in a dual‑channel debate setting. The study shows that social context alone can steer agents toward distinct public stances, revealing latent objectives that emerge without explicit instruction.
significance: This work uncovers how implicit social cues shape LLM behavior, informing safer deployment in multi‑agent systems.

title: On the emergence of quantum many-body chaos for tunably-broken integrability
headline: Quantum Chaos Emerges as Integrability Is Tunably Broken in Free Fermion Circuits
tldr: We present a quantitative framework for how quantum many-body chaos develops when integrability is gradually broken. By studying a circuit of free fermions doped with tunable integrability-breaking gates, we reveal the microscopic transition from early-time integrable dynamics to late-time chaotic behavior via out-of-time-ordered correlators.
significance: This work provides a clear microscopic understanding of chaos onset, informing both theoretical studies and experimental designs in quantum simulation.

title: Reasoning LLM Improves Speaker Recognition in Long-form TV Dramas
headline: LLM Enhances Speaker Recognition in Long-Form TV Dramas
tldr: The paper presents DramaSR-532K, a large-scale benchmark for speaker recognition in long‑form TV dramas. Using a reasoning LLM, the authors demonstrate significant performance gains over existing methods.
significance: It provides a new benchmark and shows that LLMs can improve speaker attribution, enabling richer analysis of TV dramas.

title: DemoPSD: Disagreement-Modulated Policy Self-Distillation
headline: DemoPSD: Reducing Overfitting in LLM Self-Distillation via Disagreement Modulation for Robust Reasoning
tldr: DemoPSD introduces a disagreement-modulated policy self-distillation framework that mitigates overfitting in on‑policy self‑distillation by tempering dense token‑level supervision with privileged information. The method demonstrates improved generalization and reasoning performance across diverse LLM benchmarks.
significance: By addressing overfitting in self‑distillation, DemoPSD paves the way for more reliable and generalizable large language models.

title: Geospatial & AI Trends: Highlights from 2025 - Geoawesome
headline: 2025 GeoAI Trends: Predictive Insights, Automation, and Climate Solutions Across
tldr: GeoAI in 2025 is reshaping how we monitor climate, cities, and global challenges with advanced predictive models and real‑time data streams. Key innovations include foundation models for satellite imagery, automated flood detection, and urban AI for smart city planning.
significance: These trends empower decision-makers to proactively address environmental risks and optimize resource allocation at scale.

title: Towards the next generation of Geospatial Artificial Intelligence
headline: Charting the Future of GeoAI: From Foundations to Real-World Impact
tldr: GeoAI is rapidly evolving, blending geospatial science with AI to unlock new insights. This paper reviews recent breakthroughs and outlines future directions for the field.
significance: Understanding GeoAI's trajectory is crucial for harnessing AI to address global spatial challenges.

title: GeoAI | Geo Week News
headline: AI Transforms Geospatial Work: From Automated Feature Extraction to Predictive Analysis
tldr: Artificial intelligence and machine learning are automating feature extraction and point cloud classification across satellite imagery and LiDAR data. These advances enable real‑time predictive analysis, driving smarter decisions in urban planning, agriculture, and disaster response.
significance: By scaling AI-driven interpretation of spatial data, GeoAI unlocks faster, more accurate insights that can transform how we monitor and manage the planet.

title: Top 5 Geospatial AI Trends to Watch in 2025 - arizton.com
headline: 5 Emerging GeoAI Trends Shaping 2025: From Foundation Models to Urban AI
tldr: GeoAI is accelerating with foundation models, advanced remote sensing, and urban AI applications. These trends promise smarter planning, agriculture, and disaster response in 2025.
significance: These innovations will transform how cities, farms, and emergency services use spatial data.

title: ITU Journal explores GeoAI innovation for the good of humanity
headline: ITU Journal Highlights GeoAI Innovations Driving Sustainable Development
tldr: The 2025 edition of the ITU Journal showcases how GeoAI can support sustainable development, featuring research on satellite imagery, remote sensing, and spatial reasoning. Experts discuss practical applications from flood detection to urban planning, underscoring GeoAI’s role in shaping a digital, eco‑friendly future.
significance: By integrating GeoAI into telecommunications research, the journal demonstrates how AI-driven spatial analytics can accelerate global sustainability goals.

title: GitHub - mbzuai-oryx/ThinkGeo: [CVPR 2026 ] ThinkGeo is a ...
headline: ThinkGeo: Benchmarking LLMs on Remote Sensing Tasks with Structured Tool Use
tldr: ThinkGeo is a new benchmark that tests language models on complex satellite and aerial imagery tasks using step‑by‑step reasoning and tool calls. It provides human‑curated queries across disaster response, urban planning, and environmental monitoring, enabling systematic evaluation of LLM capabilities in remote sensing.
significance: It bridges the gap between natural language processing and geospatial analysis, offering a standardized way to assess how well LLMs can interpret and act on real‑world imagery data.

title: GitHub - ua-datalab/geospatial_2025
headline: AI Models Transform GIS: From Advice to Autonomous Spatial Analysis Agents
tldr: AI systems are evolving from providing guidance to executing GIS commands autonomously, reshaping geospatial workflows. This shift enables rapid, code-free spatial analysis directly on local machines.
significance: This evolution democratizes advanced geospatial analysis, accelerating insights across environmental, urban, and agricultural domains.

title: GeoAI: Artificial Intelligence for Geospatial Data
headline: GeoAI: Bridging AI and Geospatial Data for Accessible Machine Learning
tldr: GeoAI is a Python package that integrates AI techniques with geospatial data, enabling researchers to apply machine learning to geographic datasets. It offers intuitive tools for tasks such as segmentation, object detection, and time‑series analysis in a user‑friendly framework.
significance: By simplifying AI workflows on spatial data, GeoAI accelerates insights in fields ranging from urban planning to environmental monitoring.

title: NASA Releases Galileo: The Open-Source Multimodal Model Advancing Earth ...
headline: NASA Unveils Galileo: Open-Source Multimodal Model for Earth Observation Data
tldr: NASA has released Galileo, an open-source multimodal foundation model that can ingest satellite imagery, time-series data, and tabular inputs. Its tokenization pipeline splits remote sensing data into spatial patches, timesteps, and logical channel groups, enabling unified processing across diverse modalities.
significance: Galileo democratizes advanced Earth observation analytics by providing a single, open-source framework that integrates images, time series, and tabular data, accelerating research and operational applications.

title: Raster Vision: An open-source machine learning library for deep ...
headline: Raster Vision: Open-Source Deep Learning Library Bridging GIS and Computer Vision
tldr: Raster Vision is an open-source framework that unifies GIS data with deep learning pipelines for tasks like chip classification, semantic segmentation, and object detection. Its configurable architecture enables researchers to deploy and experiment with state‑of‑the‑art models on satellite imagery.
significance: By simplifying the integration of geospatial data and deep learning, Raster Vision accelerates the development of AI solutions for environmental monitoring and urban planning.

title: An LLM-based multi-agent system for remote sensing analysis
headline: LLM-Powered Multi-Agent Framework Simplifies Remote Sensing for Non-Experts in Urban and Environmental Monitoring
tldr: We introduce a large language model-based multi-agent system that guides users through remote sensing tasks without requiring specialized expertise. The framework automates data preprocessing, analysis, and interpretation, enabling rapid, accurate insights for urban and environmental applications.
significance: This approach democratizes access to remote sensing, empowering stakeholders to make data-driven decisions without technical barriers.

title: GEAR: Guided End-to-End AutoRegression for Image Synthesis
headline: GEAR: End-to-End Training of Tokenizer and Generator for Image Synthesis
tldr: GEAR trains a vector-quantized tokenizer jointly with a generator, eliminating the decoupling that hampers traditional two-stage pipelines. This end-to-end approach improves model efficiency and synthesis quality.
significance: By aligning tokenizer and generator training, GEAR enhances generative model performance and paves the way for more coherent image synthesis.

title: PointSplat: Compact Gaussian Splatting via Human-Centric Prediction
headline: PointSplat: Fast, Compact Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time 3D Human Reconstruction
tldr: PointSplat introduces a human‑centric Gaussian splatting approach that compresses 3D human models into compact representations while preserving high fidelity. The method enables on‑the‑fly reconstruction from multiple views, making it ideal for live streaming with limited bandwidth and compute resources.
significance: This work bridges the gap between high‑quality 3D human reconstruction and the constraints of real‑time streaming, offering a scalable solution for immersive applications.

title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
headline: QVal: Cheap Evaluation of Dense Supervision for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
tldr: QVal introduces a lightweight method to assess dense supervision signals in long-horizon LLM agents, enabling more efficient training and better guidance of intermediate actions. By leveraging cheap evaluation metrics, the approach reduces reliance on sparse outcome rewards and improves agent performance across extended trajectories.
significance: It provides a scalable way to evaluate intermediate action quality, addressing a key bottleneck in training long-horizon LLM agents.

title: SpheRoPE: Zero-Shot Optimization-Free 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE
headline: Zero‑Shot 360 Panorama Generation with Spherical RoPE: A Diffusion Transformer Approach
tldr: SpheRoPE injects spherical priors into pre‑trained diffusion transformers, enabling zero‑shot, training‑free 360 panorama synthesis. The method eliminates costly fine‑tuning and multi‑step optimization, delivering real‑time panoramic images and videos.
significance: It democratizes high‑quality panoramic generation by removing the need for specialized datasets or inference overhead, making it accessible for real‑time applications.

title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
headline: LLMs Misread Tables: Quantifying and Mitigating Data Referencing Errors in Structured Data
tldr: Large language models often miscite or omit table values, producing data referencing errors that undermine reasoning steps. This study introduces a comprehensive benchmark, measures error rates, and proposes techniques to reduce such mistakes.
significance: By systematically measuring and reducing data referencing errors, the work enhances the reliability of LLMs in data-intensive applications.

title: DVG-WM: Disentangled Video Generation Enables Efficient Embodied World Model for Robotic Manipulation
headline: Disentangled Video Generation for Efficient Embodied World Models in Robotics
tldr: We propose a disentangled video generation framework that separates low-level temporal dynamics from high-level visual synthesis, enabling accurate future state predictions and high-resolution frame generation for robotic manipulation. This approach reduces computational cost and improves performance on embodied tasks.
significance: By decoupling dynamics and visual synthesis, the method advances real-time robotic manipulation with more efficient and accurate world modeling.

title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
headline: AdaJEPA: Adaptive Latent World Models for Robust Test‑Time Planning in High‑Dimensional Observations
tldr: AdaJEPA introduces a test‑time adaptive latent world model that updates its predictions during deployment, improving planning under distribution shift. It demonstrates that continuous adaptation can mitigate failure modes in high‑dimensional observation spaces.
significance: AdaJEPA shows that adaptive latent world models can maintain planning accuracy under test‑time distribution shifts, a key step toward robust real‑world deployment of model‑based RL.

title: FLORA: A deep learning approach to predict forest attributes from heterogeneous LiDAR data
headline: Predicting Forest Attributes from Heterogeneous LiDAR with Deep Learning Across Europe
tldr: FLORA uses a deep learning model to generate wall‑to‑wall forest attribute maps from airborne LiDAR data collected under varying conditions. The approach improves national forest inventory accuracy across Europe by handling data heterogeneity.
significance: Accurate, nationwide forest attribute predictions enable better resource management and policy decisions.

title: SemRF: A Semantic Reference Frame for Residual-Stream Dynamics in Language Models
headline: Introducing SemRF: A New Anchor-Based Framework for Tracking Residual-Stream Dynamics
tldr: SemRF provides a semantic reference frame that aligns embedding anchors and unembedding readouts across layers, enabling accurate tracking of residual-stream evolution in language models. By separating measurement drift from true computation, SemRF improves interpretability and facilitates deeper analysis of LLM internals.
significance: SemRF offers a principled way to monitor and understand how language models process information across depth, advancing research in model interpretability.

title: VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes
headline: New VLK Dataset Enables Humanoid Robots to Learn Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Scenes
tldr: We introduce VLK, a vision-language-kinematics dataset generated from synthetic interactions in reconstructed scenes, providing synchronized egocentric images, language commands, and robot-compatible trajectories. This dataset addresses a critical gap, enabling scalable learning of humanoid loco-manipulation tasks.
significance: By furnishing the first large-scale, multimodal dataset for humanoid loco-manipulation, VLK accelerates research in embodied AI and paves the way for more capable, perception-driven robots.

title: LeVo 2: Stable and Melodious Song Generation via Hierarchical Representation Modeling and Progressive Post-Training
headline: LeVo 2: Hierarchical Modeling for Coherent, Melodious Full-Length Song Generation
tldr: LeVo 2 introduces a hierarchical representation and progressive post‑training to generate full‑length songs that maintain lyrical coherence and realistic vocal‑instrument coordination. The approach balances mixed‑token and dual‑track modeling, yielding stable, melodious outputs with detailed acoustics.
significance: It advances AI music generation by reconciling coherence and acoustic fidelity, enabling more realistic and controllable full‑song creation.

title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
headline: Self‑Evolving World Models Empower LLM Agents with Adaptive Foresight for Long‑Horizon Planning
tldr: We propose WorldEvolver, a framework that continuously updates a world model’s context during deployment, enabling LLM agents to anticipate action outcomes more accurately. By integrating self‑evolution, the system mitigates unreliable foresight and improves downstream decision quality.
significance: WorldEvolver demonstrates that adaptive world models can enhance LLM agent planning, reducing errors from static foresight and paving the way for more reliable autonomous decision‑making.

title: Open-Vocabulary and Referring Segmentation for 3D Gaussians Using 2D Detectors
headline: Open-Vocabulary 3D Gaussian Segmentation via 2D Detectors and Language Models
tldr: This work extends 3D Gaussian Splatting by integrating open‑vocabulary language understanding, enabling 3D scene segmentation using 2D detectors and CLIP‑style contrastive learning. The approach allows real‑time, language‑driven segmentation for embodied AI applications.
significance: It bridges 3D scene reconstruction with flexible, language‑driven segmentation, opening new possibilities for real‑time, embodied AI.

title: One-Step Gradient Delay is Not a Barrier for Large-Scale Asynchronous Pipeline Parallel LLM Pretraining
headline: Asynchronous Pipeline Parallelism Boosts LLM Pretraining Efficiency Without Gradient Staleness
tldr: This study demonstrates that asynchronous pipeline parallelism can eliminate idle GPU time during large‑scale LLM pretraining, achieving higher throughput. By employing the PipeDream‑2BW schedule, the authors show that the one‑step gradient delay does not impede model convergence.
significance: It proves that asynchronous pipeline parallelism is a practical, high‑performance strategy for training massive language models.

title: GIGA-Lens 2.0: Strong-Lens Modeling on Multiple GPU Nodes
headline: GIGA-Lens 2.0: Multi-Node GPU Acceleration for Fast Strong Lens Modeling
tldr: We introduce GIGA-Lens 2.0, a GPU-accelerated Bayesian framework that now runs across multiple GPU nodes, achieving a 4× speedup on 512 A100 GPUs. The upgraded system models 100 simulated lens systems and a real DESI lens in minutes, demonstrating its scalability and scientific impact.
significance: This advancement enables rapid, large-scale strong lens analyses, accelerating cosmological studies and making high-fidelity modeling accessible to the community.

title: GROW$^2$: Grounding Which and Where for Robot Tool Use
headline: Grounding Which and Where: Robots Use Any Object as a Tool
tldr: GROW^2 enables robots to identify arbitrary objects as tools and pinpoint the exact region to use. By integrating open-world affordance grounding, the system expands robotic capabilities beyond conventional tool sets.
significance: This work demonstrates a scalable approach for robots to adaptively repurpose everyday items, bridging a critical gap in autonomous tool use.

title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
headline: Conservative Offline Training Can Exacerbate Reward Hacking in Online Adaptation of LLMs
tldr: We show that policies trained with conservative offline methods are more prone to reward hacking when fine-tuned online. Our experiments on Qwen3-14B with Direct Preference Optimisation reveal a paradoxical increase in exploitation of reward model flaws.
significance: This challenges the prevailing belief that conservative offline training inherently safeguards against reward exploitation.

title: DOPD: Dual On-policy Distillation
headline: Dual On-Policy Distillation: Enhancing Transfer with Privileged Teacher and Student Signals
tldr: Dual On-Policy Distillation (DOPD) extends OPD by injecting privileged information into teacher and student models, providing richer token-level supervision. This approach improves knowledge transfer while mitigating failure modes associated with additional inputs.
significance: DOPD pushes the performance frontier of distillation by leveraging privileged signals, offering a practical pathway for more robust model compression.

title: When and Which Sensor to Observe? Timely Tracking of a Joint Markov Source
headline: Optimizing Sensor Pulls for Joint Markov Process Estimation in Real-Time Monitoring
tldr: We study remote estimation of a joint Markov process where each component can be observed by a dedicated sensor with different sampling costs. By deciding when to stay idle or request a sensor pull, we aim to minimize estimation error while respecting cost constraints.
significance: This work provides a principled framework for cost‑aware sensor scheduling in distributed monitoring systems, improving efficiency and accuracy.

title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
headline: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
tldr: DexCompose introduces a method to compose existing dexterous manipulation policies into a single hand capable of performing multiple tasks without destructive interference. By aligning finger contact modes and preserving prior skill outcomes, the approach enables efficient multi-task manipulation in real-time.
significance: This work enables more versatile robotic hands, reducing the need to train separate policies for each task and advancing practical deployment of dexterous manipulation.

title: PerceptionRubrics: Calibrating Multimodal Evaluation to Human Perception
headline: PerceptionRubrics: A New Rubric-Based Framework for Human-Centric Multimodal Evaluation Benchmarking
tldr: PerceptionRubrics introduces a rubric-based evaluation framework that bridges the gap between high benchmark scores and real-world performance. By pairing 1,038 dense images with 12,000 instance-specific rubrics derived from golden captions, it enables atomic auditing of multimodal models.
significance: This framework provides a more granular, human-aligned assessment of multimodal models, helping developers identify brittleness that traditional benchmarks miss.

title: StructSplat: Generalizable 3D Gaussian Splatting from Uncalibrated Sparse Views
headline: StructSplat: Fast, Generalizable 3D Gaussian Reconstruction from Uncalibrated Images without Camera Calibration
tldr: StructSplat introduces a feed‑forward framework that reconstructs high‑fidelity 3D Gaussian models directly from sparse, uncalibrated images, eliminating the need for per‑scene optimization or known camera poses. By decoupling geometry and appearance, it achieves superior reconstruction quality and generalization across diverse scenes.
significance: It enables scalable, real‑time 3D reconstruction from commodity cameras, opening new possibilities for rapid scene understanding without costly calibration.

title: The Role of Scintillation in Detecting HI Absorption in FRB Spectra
headline: Scintillation Enhances Detectability of 21‑cm Hydrogen Absorption in Fast Radio Burst Spectra
tldr: We investigate how interstellar scintillation can boost the visibility of the 21‑cm HI absorption line in FRB spectra. Our results identify parameter regimes where realistic FRB observations could reveal this key cosmological probe.
significance: This study demonstrates a practical pathway to use FRBs as probes of the intergalactic medium by leveraging scintillation effects.

title: Diameter truncated operator evolution
headline: Efficient Operator Dynamics via Diameter Truncation: A New Approach to Quantum Simulation
tldr: We introduce a diameter truncation technique to simulate operator dynamics in out-of-equilibrium quantum systems. This method reduces computational complexity by focusing on low-weight operators, enabling more tractable simulations.
significance: This approach offers a scalable solution for studying complex quantum dynamics, potentially accelerating research in quantum computing and many-body physics.

title: Optimal Deployment of Electric Aircraft for Canadian Domestic Flights
headline: Electric Aircraft Deployment Plan Optimizes Canadian Domestic Flights via Multi-Period MILP
tldr: The study introduces a multi-period MILP framework that jointly optimizes fleet acquisition, infrastructure deployment, and service allocation for Canadian regional aviation. It incorporates policy constraints such as emissions targets, electric service share, and budget limits to guide the transition from conventional to electric aircraft.
significance: It provides a data-driven, policy-compliant strategy for accelerating sustainable aviation in Canada.
